senses.definition: A component in plastic-processing machinery consisting of a helical screw inside a barrel that transports, melts, and mixes material during extrusion or molding.
examples: The factory upgraded the screwbarrel to improve plastic melting uniformity.
Maintenance routine includes cleaning the screwbarrel after each batch.

senses.definition: A leguminous shrub or small tree (Strombocarpa pubescens) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for spirally twisted pods with sweet pulp.
examples: Screwbean was once cultivated for its edible pods.
The screwbean tree produces distinctive twisted pods.

senses.definition: Plural form of screwbean; the twisted pods of the screwbean plant or the plant itself in plural reference.
examples: The field yielded many screwbeans this season.
Screwbeans are harvested carefully to avoid pod damage.

senses.definition: The internal mechanism inside a screw or drill that translates rotational motion into linear movement, typically a helical screw inside a barrel or head.
examples: The screwdrive alignment ensures the bit fits securely.
Different screwdrives are used for various head shapes.

senses.definition: A tool used for turning screws; can be manual or powered.
examples: He grabbed a screwdriver to tighten the loose screw.
A cordless screwdriver makes assembly faster.

senses.definition: Plural of screwdriver; multiple tools used for turning screws.
examples: The toolbox contains several screwdrivers of different sizes.
He bought a set of screwdrivers with interchangeable bits.

senses.definition: Held, fixed, or fastened with screws; also used informally to mean in trouble or ruined.
examples: The panel is screwed onto the frame.
If the plan goes wrong, we're screwed.

senses.definition: A person who screws or fastens with a screwdriver; can be informal or colloquial for someone who causes trouble (slang).
examples: The screwer tightened the bolt.
Don't be a screwer—follow the plan.

senses.definition: Plural of screwer; people or devices that screw or fasten.
examples: The team of screwers completed the frame assembly.
Various screwers were used for different materials.

senses.definition: A species of fly in the genus Orthellia or related taxa known for laying eggs on materials; may also refer to a common name used in specific crafts or contexts.
examples: Screwflies emerged near the workshop after the rain.
Researchers studied the life cycle of the screwfly.

senses.definition: The top part of a screw that is driven by a screwdriver or drill.
examples: The screwhead was damaged and needed replacement.
Choose the correct screwdriver for the screwhead shape.

senses.definition: More ridiculous or more difficult to deal with; used figuratively to describe things that seem increasingly ridiculous or severe.
examples: The situation is getting screwier by the minute.
That plan sounds even screwier than before.

senses.definition: Most ridiculous or most difficult to deal with; superlative of screwier.
examples: That theory is the screwiest thing I've heard.
This assignment is the screwiest of all.

senses.definition: The quality of being strange, ridiculous, or suspicious; oddness.
examples: There was a lot of screwiness in the plan.
The screwiness of the situation made us nervous.

senses.definition: plural form of screwiness; multiple instances of oddness or suspiciousness.
examples: The report contained several screwinesses worth questioning.
We noted various screwinesses across the documents.

senses.definition: The act of using a screwdriver to fasten or remove screws; can refer to the process.
examples: Screwing the bolts tightened the frame.
He spent the afternoon screwing and unscrewing small screws.

senses.definition: To fasten with screws; to twist or turn to secure.
examples: She is screwing the plate onto the wall.
Don't over-screw the bolt.

senses.definition: Plural of screwing; instances of fastening or turning screws.
examples: The toolbox contained several screwings for different materials.
Record each screwing to track maintenance.

senses.definition: Having the character or quality of screwiness; odd or suspicious in a light, informal way.
examples: The plan had a screwish vibe to it.
That proposal feels a bit screwish to me.

senses.definition: Having no screws; not fastened with screws.
examples: The damper was screwless and needed a new fastener.
A screwless joint can be less secure.

senses.definition: Resembling or having the form of a screw; twisted or helical in shape.
examples: The screwlike pattern on the bolt helps with grip.
Use a screwlike thread to ensure proper engagement.

senses.definition: A worker who installs or handles screws; historically a male worker in a workshop.
examples: The screwman finished tightening the chassis.
Screwman duties include assembling parts and checking screws.

senses.definition: A coined or rare plural noun; may refer to systems or devices involving screws in a broad sense.
examples: The machine's screwmatics require recalibration.
Screwmatics are central to this assembly line.

senses.definition: A pile (geotechnical foundation element) with a screw-like anchoring mechanism; used for footing in soft ground.
examples: The screwpile foundation reduced sinking on marshy soil.
Engineers installed several screwpiles along the shoreline.

senses.definition: A plate with screws or screw-related fittings; used to attach components or as part of a mechanism.
examples: The screwplate secured the plates together.
Replace the worn screwplate before reassembly.

senses.definition: A pod or container designed to be secured or attached with screws; may refer to a modular component in a larger assembly.
examples: The screwpod attaches to the frame with four screws.
Industrial designers used screwpods for modularity.
